target_audience.primary_persona: Privacy-conscious individual who wants a fast, no-signup task manager they can use immediately in the browser
secondary_personas: Minimalist productivity seekers who distrust data-hungry apps
Casual users needing a quick personal to-do list without installing anything
Developers or technical users who appreciate a self-contained, zero-dependency tool
pain_points: Popular task apps require account creation before use
Most task managers collect user data and use tracking/analytics
Feature-bloated apps are overwhelming for simple task tracking
Many apps require internet connectivity to function
Task data is stored on third-party servers without user control
goals: Track daily tasks without friction or sign-up barriers
Keep personal task data private and local to their device
Quickly add, edit, complete, and delete tasks in a clean interface
Access their task list on mobile and desktop seamlessly
Have tasks persist across browser sessions
target_audience.usage_context: Daily personal task management, accessed via browser on both mobile and desktop. Used throughout the day to add, check off, and review tasks. No backend dependency — fully offline-capable.

product_vision.one_liner: A zero-tracking, no-account-needed todo app that lets you start managing tasks instantly in your browser.
product_vision.problem_statement: Most task management apps require account creation, collect user data, and come loaded with features most users never need — creating unnecessary friction and privacy concerns for people who just want a simple, private place to track their tasks.
product_vision.value_proposition: My Todo List is the fastest way to start tracking tasks: open the page, type a task, press Add. No sign-up, no data collection, no external requests — your tasks stay on your device via LocalStorage, guaranteed.
success_metrics: User can add their first task in under 10 seconds from page load
Zero third-party network requests made by the app
Tasks persist correctly across page refreshes and browser restarts
App is fully functional on mobile viewports (375px and above)
All CRUD operations (add, edit, complete, delete) work without errors
No personal data ever leaves the user's device

existing_features: Add new tasks via text input (button click or Enter key)
List all tasks in a clean card-based UI
Toggle task completion via checkbox (visual strikethrough for completed tasks)
Edit task title inline with Save/Cancel and keyboard shortcuts (Enter/Escape)
Filter tasks by All / Active / Completed
Accessible ARIA labels on interactive elements
Zero external dependencies — fully self-contained single HTML file
Privacy-first design with explicit no-tracking policy in code comments
known_gaps: No user authentication or multi-device sync
No due dates or deadlines for tasks
No bulk actions (e.g., clear all completed)
No task count or progress summary display
No keyboard shortcut to focus input from anywhere on the page
technical_debt: All code (HTML, CSS, JS) lives in a single file — no separation of concerns
No build system or tooling (no bundler, linter, or formatter configured)
render() does a full DOM re-render on every change — no virtual DOM or diffing
No error boundary for LocalStorage quota exceeded scenarios
No input sanitization beyond basic trim (XSS safe via textContent but worth documenting)

differentiators: Zero-tracking guarantee enforced in code — no analytics, cookies, or external requests; auditable via source code (unlike any SaaS competitor)
No account or sign-up required — open in browser, add a task in under 10 seconds (no competitor can match sub-10-second onboarding without an account)
Entirely self-contained single HTML file — can be saved locally and run offline without any server
No external CDN or third-party script dependencies
Extremely lightweight — loads instantly with no network round-trips
Forever free, all features included — no paywalled tiers unlike Todoist, TickTick, and Any.do
Deterministic local task ordering — no sync conflicts or random reordering (contrast with TickTick bugs)
Full data ownership — tasks never leave the device; no foreign/domestic server ever receives task data (addresses TickTick China-server concerns)
competitive_context.market_position: Positioned as the privacy-first, zero-friction alternative to mainstream task apps. Occupies a clear market gap: every major competitor requires sign-up and sends task data to third-party servers. My Todo List targets the growing segment of privacy-aware users fatigued by freemium paywalls, mandatory accounts, and data collection — competing on trust, immediacy, and data ownership rather than collaboration or power features.
competitor_pain_points: Mandatory account creation before first use — all 5 major competitors require sign-up
Privacy and data collection concerns — user tasks stored on third-party servers, shared with analytics and ad platforms
Feature bloat overwhelming casual users who just need a simple checklist
Paywalled basic features (reminders, recurring tasks, filters) frustrating free-tier users
Sync reliability issues causing lost tasks, ordering bugs, and multi-device inconsistencies
Vendor lock-in — task data tied to proprietary accounts with limited export options

technical: LocalStorage is limited to ~5MB per origin — not suitable for large task datasets
LocalStorage is browser/device-specific — no cross-device sync without a backend
Single HTML file architecture limits code scalability for future feature growth
No build tooling means adding TypeScript, bundling, or testing requires setup investment
Vanilla JS DOM manipulation becomes harder to maintain as features grow
resources: Appears to be a solo developer project based on project structure
No package.json indicates minimal tooling investment — likely a small or individual team
MVP scope suggests a focused, time-boxed development effort
dependencies: Browser LocalStorage API (available in all modern browsers)
No external APIs, services, or CDN dependencies
No server infrastructure required for current version
